description: Fix task ordering system and group filtering bug - these are critical bugs that must be resolved before redesigning the UI
steps: In src/App.jsx, fix the reorderTask function (line ~3099). Current issue: when two tasks have the same sortOrder (e.g., both 0 or both undefined), swapping them produces no visible change. Fix: instead of swapping sortOrder values, reassign sequential sortOrder values to the section after swap. Also ensure sortOrder is always initialized - existing tasks loaded from localStorage may have sortOrder=0 or undefined for all tasks.

In src/App.jsx, fix the addTask function (line ~3066). The sortOrder calculation `Math.max(0, ...prev.map(t => (t.sortOrder || 0))) + 1` works for new tasks but does not account for tasks within the same group/section needing sequential ordering. Ensure each new task gets a unique sortOrder.

Fix group filtering bug: In src/TaskPanel.jsx (line ~188 in the useMemo sections), the filter `task.groupId === activeGroupFilter` fails for tasks that have undefined groupId. These tasks should be treated as belonging to 'inbox'. The fix is to normalize: `(task.groupId || 'inbox') === activeGroupFilter`. Check the current code - it already uses `(task.groupId || 'inbox')` on line ~188, but verify the App.jsx import flow: in handleImport (line ~2154), imported tasks may not have groupId set. Add a migration step when loading tasks that ensures every task has a groupId field defaulting to 'inbox'.

In src/App.jsx, add a migration/normalization step in the initialization code (around line 695 where tasks are loaded from saved state). After loading tasks, ensure each task has: groupId (default 'inbox'), sortOrder (assign sequential values if missing/all-zeros). This ensures persistence across refresh/restart/import.
Verify the reorderTask function properly handles the case where tasks have identical sortOrder values by re-indexing the section's tasks with sequential sortOrder before performing the swap.
acceptance_criteria: Moving a task up/down immediately changes its position in the list
Task order persists after page refresh (localStorage save/load cycle preserves order)
Tasks without a groupId display correctly under 'Inbox' group filter
Selecting a specific group filter shows all tasks belonging to that group without requiring edits
Newly imported tasks (from JSON files that may lack groupId) appear correctly under Inbox
npm run build succeeds without errors
verification: Run `npm run build` to confirm no compilation errors
